summary: High‑energy MSG show with killer Candyman, Eyes jam, soaring Attics
blurb: The first set packs a punch with Touch of Grey and Candyman, while the second set expands into an epic Eyes Of The World that flows through Saint Of Circumstance, Drums and The Other One. The night ends on a soaring Attics Of My Life encore. Jerry’s guitar is fiery, Phil’s bass drives the groove, and the rhythm section is fierce.
review: On September 8 1991 at Madison Square Garden the Dead delivered a high‑octane performance that still feels fresh. A song‑rich first set launched with the sing‑along optimism of Touch Of Grey before diving into a fiery Candyman and a rollicking Row Jimmy, setting up a solid groove that carried through Let It Grow. The second set was where the magic unfolded – Eyes Of The World opened a deep, soaring jam that melted into Saint Of Circumstance, a tight Drums/Space segment, and an inspired stretch of The Other One that kept the audience on its feet. The band closed with a heartfelt Wharf Rat segue into Good Lovin' and capped the night with the soaring beauty of Attics Of My Life. Reviewers rave about the crystal‑clear soundboard capture (ashley’s SBD), noting Jerry’s spirited guitar work, Phil’s tasteful bass lines, and a fierce dual‑drum attack from Mickey and Bill that powered the extended improvisations. Keyboard textures from Bruce Hornsby (and Vince Welnick) added extra sparkle on Candyman and throughout the set. While a few songs like Saint, Big River, Maggie’s Farm and Good Lovin’ were seen as weaker spots, the overall energy and musicianship make this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive (high‑energy performance, standout jams) and one is mixed but still praises the Saint/Drums segment; no consistent negative comments about playing were found, so a moderate positive adjustment of +1.2 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with a loud, clear, well‑balanced mix that captures the high energy and all key jams without dropouts.

key_highlights: High‑energy first set highlighted by Candyman and tight rhythm work.
Epic Eyes Of The World jam leading into Saint Of Circumstance and a powerful Drums/Space segment.
Soaring Attics Of My Life encore that lifts the whole night.
Strong bass presence and fierce dual‑drum attack driving the improvisations.

band_performance.Jerry: Spirited guitar work and vocal energy, with an early Bob‑style line on Touch of Grey; voice slips a bit on Good Lovin' but overall fiery throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Great taste on bass, driving the groove especially during Touch of Grey and throughout the jam-heavy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Amusing early vocal entry on Touch of Grey and solid rhythm guitar work; occasional vocal fatigue noted on Good Lovin'.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard textures (Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick) receive strong praise, adding sparkle to Candyman and enhancing the overall sound.
band_performance.Mickey: Fierce and dynamic drumming, powering the killer Eyes jam and post‑drum energy in the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Dynamic percussion complementing Mickey, delivering a powerful Drums segment and driving the extended improvisations.
